free_text: (gamma,p) cross sections and isospin splitting of the
giant dipole resonance in N = 50 nuclei.
reaction differential cross  section  obtaining from
(e,e'p) reaction cross sections measured using  the
direct proton detection with the following
90Zr, and 92Mo have been measured at theta = 90 degr.
The results are used to obtain the (gamma,p) cross
sections by correcting with the angular distribution
data.  The  (gamma,p)  cross  sections  are compared
with the (gamma,n) cross sections.  A resonance
corresponding to the t < GDR  is  found  and  another
resonance is separated by fitting a Lorenz line with
a width equal to that of the (gamma,n) GDR. The
between the two resonances. The two GDR resonances
are studied from the isospin splitting point of view.
The sum rule and splitting energy of the (gamma,p)
GDR agree well with theory. However, when the sum
is taken, the  results are too large to be explained
